name: clinicaltrials_study_scraper
title: ClinicalTrials.gov Study Scraper
description: Search ClinicalTrials.gov studies or fetch one exact NCT record through the public ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API, returning normalized trial metadata, status, sponsors, conditions, interventions, phases, enrollment, dates, eligibility, outcomes, and locations.

seo.title: ClinicalTrials.gov Study Scraper Tool for Trial Data
seo.description: Search ClinicalTrials.gov studies or fetch exact NCT IDs with Better Fetch via the public v2 API, including trial status, sponsors, conditions, interventions, phases, enrollment, dates, eligibility, outcomes, and locations.
seo.intro: ClinicalTrials.gov Study Scraper gives agents a structured route into the official ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API. Search by condition, intervention, sponsor, location, or free-text term, filter by overall status or supported aggregate filters, or fetch a known NCT record directly. The tool normalizes the nested API response into compact trial rows with NCT IDs, study URLs, titles, current status, result availability, conditions, interventions, phases, study type, enrollment, sponsors, important dates, eligibility criteria, outcome measures, countries, and locations. It is designed for clinical-trial monitoring, pharma pipeline research, patient-recruitment discovery, competitive intelligence, medical literature triage, and research-agent workflows that need reliable public trial metadata without scraping the website.

useCases.title: Recruiting trial discovery
useCases.description: Find currently recruiting studies for a disease, intervention, sponsor, or location and return normalized trial rows for review.

useCases.title: Pharma pipeline monitoring
useCases.description: Track sponsor, condition, phase, enrollment, result availability, start dates, completion dates, and last update dates from official records.

useCases.title: Patient and site research
useCases.description: Collect eligibility snippets, contacts-ready study URLs, countries, and facility locations before deeper manual screening.

faqs.question: Does ClinicalTrials.gov Study Scraper require an API key?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses the public ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API and keeps each run to one bounded request. Large exports should page through results politely with the returned next_page_token.

faqs.question: Can it filter by clinical-trial phase?
faqs.answer: Yes, through the agg_filters input using ClinicalTrials.gov aggregate filter syntax, for example phase:3. The normalized output always includes the phase labels reported by each returned study.

faqs.question: Is this tool affiliated with ClinicalTrials.gov or NIH?
faqs.answer: No. This Better Fetch tool uses public ClinicalTrials.gov API data and is not endorsed by or affiliated with ClinicalTrials.gov, NIH, NLM, or the U.S. government.

mode.description: Search ClinicalTrials.gov studies or fetch one exact NCT record.

nct_id.description: Exact NCT identifier for study mode, e.g. NCT04280705.

query.description: General free-text search across ClinicalTrials.gov study fields.

condition.description: Disease or condition query, mapped to query.cond.

intervention.description: Drug, device, procedure, or other intervention query, mapped to query.intr.

sponsor.description: Sponsor or collaborator query, mapped to query.spons.

location.description: Location query such as Boston, Massachusetts or United States, mapped to query.locn.

status.description: Overall status filter. Use API enum values such as RECRUITING, COMPLETED, ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING, NOT_YET_RECRUITING, TERMINATED, or comma-separated values.

agg_filters.description: Optional raw ClinicalTrials.gov aggregate filter expression, e.g. phase:3 or studyType:int.

page_size.description: Maximum studies to return in search mode.

page_token.description: ClinicalTrials.gov nextPageToken from a previous search response.

include_eligibility.description: Include truncated eligibility criteria text when available.

total_matches.description: Total matches reported by ClinicalTrials.gov when requested

next_page_token.description: Token for the next search page
